input.prompt:
I need a CollectionView that supports single item selection and
pull-to-refresh. When the user selects an item, I want to navigate
to a detail page. When they pull to refresh, I want to reload data
from my API. Show me the XAML and explain the ViewModel bindings needed.

I need a CollectionView that supports single item selection and
pull-to-refresh. When the user selects an item, I want to navigate
to a detail page. When they pull to refresh, I want to reload data
from my API. Show me the XAML and explain the ViewModel bindings needed.

input.initialPrompt:
I need a CollectionView that supports single item selection and
pull-to-refresh. When the user selects an item, I want to navigate
to a detail page. When they pull to refresh, I want to reload data
from my API. Show me the XAML and explain the ViewModel bindings needed.

data.content:
Here's a complete example:

**XAML (ItemsPage.xaml)**
```xml
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
             x:Class="MyApp.ItemsPage">

    <CollectionView ItemsSource="{Binding Items}"
                    SelectionMode="Single"
                    SelectedItem="{Binding SelectedItem}"
                    IsRefreshing="{Binding IsRefreshing}"
                    RemainingItemsThreshold="0">

        <CollectionView.Header>
            <RefreshView Command="{Binding RefreshCommand}"
                         IsRefreshing="{Binding IsRefreshing}">
            </RefreshView>
        </CollectionView.Header>

    </CollectionView>

</ContentPage>
```

Actually, `RefreshView` wraps the `CollectionView` â€” here's the correct structure:

**XAML (ItemsPage.xaml)**
```xml
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
             x:Class="MyApp.ItemsPage">

    <RefreshView Command="{Binding RefreshCommand}"
                 IsRefreshing="{Binding IsRefreshing}">

        <CollectionView ItemsSource="{Binding Items}"
                        SelectionMode="Single"
                        SelectedItem="{Binding SelectedItem}">
            <CollectionView.ItemTemplate>
                <DataTemplate>
                    <Grid Padding="10" ColumnDefinitions="*,Auto">
                        <Label Text="{Binding Name}"
                               FontSize="16"
                               VerticalOptions="Center" />
                        <Label Grid.Column="1"
                               Text="â€ş"
                               FontSize="20"
                               VerticalOptions="Center" />
                    </Grid>
                </DataTemplate>
            </CollectionView.ItemTemplate>
        </CollectionView>

    </RefreshView>

</ContentPage>
```

**ViewModel (ItemsViewModel.cs)**
```csharp
public partial class ItemsViewModel : ObservableObject
{
    private readonly IMyApiService _api;

    [ObservableProperty]
    private ObservableCollection<Item> _items = new();

    [ObservableProperty]
    private bool _isRefreshing;

    [ObservableProperty]
    private Item? _selectedItem;

    public ItemsViewModel(IMyApiService api) => _api = api;

    // Fires when SelectedItem changes
    partial void OnSelectedItemChanged(Item? value)
    {
        if (value is null) return;
        // Navigate, then clear selection so it can be re-tapped
        Shell.Current.GoToAsync(nameof(DetailPage),
            new Dictionary<string, object> { ["Item"] = value });
        SelectedItem = null;
    }

    [RelayCommand]
    private async Task RefreshAsync()
    {
        try
        {
            var data = await _api.GetItemsAsync();
            Items = new ObservableCollection<Item>(data);
        }
        finally
        {
            IsRefreshing = false; // stops the spinner
        }
    }
}
```

**Key bindings explained:**

| Binding | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `ItemsSource="{Binding Items}"` | Drives the list from an `ObservableCollection` |
| `SelectionMode="Single"` | Enables single-tap selection |
| `SelectedItem="{Binding SelectedItem}"` | Two-way; ViewModel reacts via `OnSelectedItemChanged` |
| `RefreshView.Command` | Triggers `RefreshAsync` on pull-down |
| `RefreshView.IsRefreshing` | Two-way; set to `false` when done to dismiss spinner |

**Important details:**
- Uses **MVVM Community Toolkit** (`[ObservableProperty]`, `[RelayCommand]`) to eliminate boilerplate.
- `SelectedItem` is reset to `null` after navigation so the same row can be tapped again.
- `IsRefreshing = false` is in a `finally` block so the spinner always stops, even on errors.

This is a general knowledge question about MAUI/XAML development - I don't need to search any codebase. I'll provide the XAML and ViewModel explanation directly.
